out_of_scope: Pause (Definition.IsPaused column not added; design accommodates future addition as one migration)
Fence tokens for non-idempotent external work (column not added to Lease; retrofittable as one migration)
DashboardPush event broadcasting for service state changes (REST polling only in v1)
Per-service ShutdownTimeout override (one global knob in v1)
StopAsync override hook on the base class
Per-instance pause (pause a specific service on a specific server)
[NoCapture] attribute for selective log-capture suppression — documented PII responsibility instead

tests.evidence: 14/14 passing across PG + SQL Server in 18s

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: Four new tables in warp schema on both PG and SQL Server: BackgroundServiceDefinition, BackgroundServiceInstance, BackgroundServiceLease, BackgroundServiceLog. FK semantics: Instance/Lease require matching Definition (restrict); Definition delete blocked while Instances exist (restrict); Instance delete cascades to its Log rows. Three new public enums under Warp.Core.BackgroundServices: ServiceScope, BackgroundServiceStatus, BackgroundServiceLogSource.

deviations.detail: No Configurations/ folder; EF configs are static methods on ServiceConfiguration.cs.
deviations.justification: Matches existing codebase pattern; sidecar amended to remove 4 phantom Configurations paths.

deviations.detail: WarpModelCustomizer.cs not modified; entity registration via EntityConfigurators opt-in list populated by AddBackgroundService<T>() in Batch 2.
deviations.justification: Matches existing addon pattern (sagas, concurrency). Sidecar amended to remove WarpModelCustomizer.cs path.

deviations.detail: Tests in EntitySchemaTestsBase.cs (not EntitySchemaTests.cs).
deviations.justification: Source generator requires `*Base` suffix to emit `_PostgreSql` and `_SqlServer` concrete classes.

tests.evidence: StateService 9/9 PG + 9/9 SQL Server; LeaseCoordinator 6/6 PG + 6/6 SQL Server; NoDb 503/503 unchanged

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: IBackgroundServiceStateService and IBackgroundServiceLeaseCoordinator now resolvable from DI in any worker host. RegisterAsync upserts Definition + Instance transactionally; returns RegistrationOutcome.ConfigurationMismatch when DeclaredScope disagrees with existing Definition row (and inserts Instance with Status=ConfigurationMismatch). SetStatus/RecordFault/ResetRestartCount/Delete operate via ExecuteUpdateAsync/ExecuteDeleteAsync scoped to @me. TryAcquireAsync handles new-lease, expired-lease, own-lease-extend, and other-server-holds branches transactionally. ReleaseAsync deletes only @me's row.

deviations.detail: Concrete generic implementations placed in Warp.Worker.BackgroundServices, not Warp.Core.BackgroundServices.
deviations.justification: WarpWorkerConfiguration (source of ServerId) lives in Warp.Worker; Warp.Core cannot reference Warp.Worker. Mirrors IWarpSqlQueries pattern (interface Core, impls in providers). Interfaces stayed in Warp.Core.BackgroundServices as planned.

deviations.detail: TryAddScoped registrations added in Warp.Worker/ServiceConfiguration.AddWarpWorkerInner instead of BackgroundServiceConfiguration.cs.
deviations.justification: Same layering reason — Warp.Core builder can't register a Warp.Worker type.

deviations.detail: RegistrationOutcome enum added (Registered=1, ConfigurationMismatch=2).
deviations.justification: Planned as a record in spec; implemented as enum for cleaner switch ergonomics. §8.11 — starts at 1.

tests.evidence: Collector 8/8 NoDb; LogRetention 4/4 PG + 4/4 SQL Server; NoDb suite 511/511; RecurringJobLog regression 8/8

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: Per-instance log capture pipeline ready: collector with rate cap (100/s → 10s drop window with synthetic Warning/Information), 4KB truncation, timer-driven batched flushes; logger provider category-filtered to the service's type FullName; lifecycle logger with named methods for Started/LeaseAcquired/LeaseLost/Faulted/Restarting/Stopped/ConfigurationMismatch. ExpirationCleanup extended with BackgroundServiceLog count cap + age cap sweeps via ExecuteDeleteAsync. IBackgroundServiceLogStore registered as scoped.

deviations.detail: IBackgroundServiceLogCollector added.
deviations.justification: Logger + lifecycle logger live in Warp.Core; collector impl in Warp.Worker. Interface in Core breaks the dependency cycle, same pattern as IBackgroundServiceLogStore.

tests.evidence: 39/39 new tests pass on PG + SQL Server; 1 intentional skip (HealthyReset, FakeTimeProvider deferred); 511/511 NoDb regression unchanged

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: Runtime live: BackgroundServiceHost<TContext> discovers WarpBackgroundService instances and spawns one BackgroundServiceSupervisor per service. Per-server uses PerServerServiceStrategy (always acquires). Singleton uses SingletonServiceStrategy<TContext> (lease-coordinated, signal-subscribed via PublishBackgroundServiceLeaseLost/SubscribeBackgroundServiceLeaseLost on ServerTaskSignals<TContext>). Supervisor: register → loop → acquire → status=Running → invoke user code → on fault: record + lifecycle-log + backoff 1→30s + healthy-reset at ≥5min. Graceful return = fault. Graceful shutdown DELETEs lease + instance rows for @me BEFORE awaiting user code. ConfigurationMismatch refuses to enter the loop. WarpWorkerConfiguration: 2 new nullable knobs (BackgroundServiceLeaseTtl, BackgroundServiceAcquirePollInterval). WarpTestServer: WaitForBackgroundServiceState helper.

deviations.detail: HealthyReset test is a Skipped stub.
deviations.justification: FakeTimeProvider plumbing for the supervisor's startedAt comparison would be a larger change than fits this batch. Logic itself is straightforward and is exercised indirectly by the RestartBackoff tests; explicit assertion deferred.

deviations.detail: WarpTestServer.WaitForBackgroundServiceState added.
deviations.justification: Sanctioned in the batch prompt; needed for deterministic assertions per §4.5 (no Task.Delay).

tests.evidence: Heartbeat regression 12/12; LeaseRenewal 8/8; LeaseLossCts 2/2; SingletonFailover 4/4; UngracefulCleanup 4/4; PG total 694/695 + 1 prior skip; SQL Server 688/689 + 1 prior skip; NoDb 511/511

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: Heartbeat task now bundles BackgroundServiceInstance heartbeat update + Lease renewal into the existing CTE/multi-statement batch; HeartbeatResult.RenewedBackgroundServiceLeases carries the renewed names; HeartbeatLeaseTracker (Singleton) holds previousHeld across Scoped Heartbeat instances; lost = previous - renewed → PublishBackgroundServiceLeaseLost. WarpServerRegistration.StopAsync deletes @me's Lease + Instance rows. ServerCleanup removes Instance + Lease for dead servers. PostgresWarpSqlQueries CTE gains instance_bump + lease_renewed + array column; SqlServerWarpSqlQueries adds DECLARE @renewed + OUTPUT INTO + NextResultAsync. Provider ServiceConfiguration forwards LeaseTtlSeconds via WarpJobTableNames.FromModel.

deviations.detail: HeartbeatLeaseTracker.cs Singleton.
deviations.justification: Heartbeat is Scoped (depends on Scoped TContext); previousHeld must survive across ticks. Tracker is in-package, no new public contract.

deviations.detail: LeaseLossCtsCancellationTests uses Task.Delay(600ms) instead of deterministic synchronization.
deviations.justification: Subagent rationale: RunHeartbeatOnceAsync creates fresh scopes that can't populate the Singleton HeartbeatLeaseTracker across calls; auto-heartbeat loop required. **This is a real §4.5 violation flagged for Phase 4 test-reviewer to address (likely via redesigning the tracker boundary or adding a test seam) + Phase 7 lesson.**

deviations.detail: SingletonFailoverTests.HolderGracefulShutdown asserts barrier-wait rather than direct lease=null check.
deviations.justification: Direct null check was racy: waiter (polling 200ms) could re-acquire before test reads DB. Barrier-wait is deterministic.

tests.evidence: DashboardQueryShape 18/18 across both backends; AddonsEndpoint 11/11 NoDb; full NoDb 513/513

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: GET /warp/api/addons returns Services flag. Four new dashboard routes: GET /warp/api/services (list aggregated per Name with Status counts + last-error type), /warp/api/services/{name} (detail with per-instance rows), /warp/api/services/{name}/logs (paginated tail with source/level/fromId/limit filters), /warp/api/services/{name}/lease (404 for per-server, lease detail for singleton). All routes return 404 when IBackgroundServiceQueryService is not registered.

deviations.detail: Endpoints inline in WarpEndpoints.cs, not separate per-endpoint files.
deviations.justification: All existing dashboard endpoints (sagas, concurrency, ratelimits) are inline in WarpEndpoints.cs. [WarpHttpGet] source generator is for IJob/IMessage HTTP endpoints, not dashboard routes.

deviations.detail: WarpAddonsInfo reordered to strict alphabetical: Concurrency, Push, RateLimits, Sagas, Services.
deviations.justification: Plan asked for alphabetical/logical ordering; tests updated to match.

build.evidence: tsc -b && vite build: 2359 modules, 0 errors, built in 1.03s

tests.evidence: no frontend unit tests by codebase convention; build success is the gate

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: Dashboard frontend: Services nav entry gated on addons.services; /warp/services list (one row per service, status summary, mismatch badge, 2s polling); /warp/services/:name detail (per-instance tabs, lease panel for singletons with countdown, log tail filterable by source + level, incremental fromId polling).

deviations.detail: pages/BackgroundServices/ PascalCase vs lowercase existing pages.
deviations.justification: Cosmetic on Windows; matches batch spec exactly. Pre-existing pages mixed.

deviations.detail: Manual tab implementation since shadcn/ui Tabs component is not installed.
deviations.justification: Adding shadcn Tabs would expand scope. Manual button-based switcher is stylistically consistent with existing collapsible sections.

deviations.detail: Initial fetch without fromId; subsequent fetches with fromId = max seen id; filter change resets cursor.
deviations.justification: Backend returns rows id > fromId; this avoids re-fetching seen rows on poll and rebuilds correctly when filters change.

tests.evidence: Full suite: 1929 total, 1927 pass, 2 deliberate skips (HealthyResetTests from Batch 5), 0 failures. BackgroundServiceTelemetry 6/6 NoDb; LogCapture 4/4 PG + 4/4 SQL Server.

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: Four OTel counters: warp.background_services.started (tag service_name), warp.background_services.faulted (tags service_name + exception_type), warp.background_services.restarts (tag service_name), warp.background_services.lease_lost (tag service_name). CLAUDE.md gains §8.18; architecture.md gains §2.13; project-specific.md gains §8.18; website/docs/features/background-services.md is the new feature doc; README gets one bullet.

deviations.detail: Faulted counter emitted in the convergent if(faultException) block, not the catch block.
deviations.justification: Graceful return path sets faultException without going through catch; emitting in catch would miss it. Convergent block is the canonical single site.

deviations.detail: UserLogAtDebug_Captured_WhenMinLogLevelDebug integration test not added.
deviations.justification: Host ILoggerFactory applies global Information minimum at the outer pipeline before reaching the provider, making the assertion structurally unreliable. BackgroundServiceLogCollectorTests (Batch 4) cover the MinLogLevel filter in isolation.

deviations.detail: LoggingService.cs added under TestData/BackgroundServices/.
deviations.justification: Needed by LogCaptureTests to emit user logs through an injected ILogger<TService> from within ExecuteAsync.

completed_batches.behavioral_diff: WarpBackgroundService abstract base class is now a public API surface in Warp.Core.BackgroundServices. AddBackgroundService<TContext,T>() builder extension registers the user service as a singleton with a WarpBackgroundService alias, contributes the four entity configurators idempotently. WarpConfiguration gains BackgroundServiceShutdownTimeout (30s), BackgroundServiceLogRetentionCount (1000), BackgroundServiceLogRetentionAge (7 days) properties. No runtime behavior yet — no host, no DB writes.

deviations.detail: BackgroundServiceConfiguration.cs (matches SagaServiceConfiguration / ConcurrencyServiceConfiguration) instead of planned WarpBackgroundServiceBuilderExtensions.cs.
deviations.justification: Codebase convention.

deviations.detail: AddBackgroundService<TContext, T>(this IWarpBuilder<TContext>) instead of planned (this WarpWorkerConfiguration opt).
deviations.justification: WarpWorkerConfiguration has no IServiceCollection; every existing addon (Sagas, Concurrency, CircuitBreaker, DatabasePush) extends IWarpBuilder<TContext>.

deviations.detail: AddSingleton factory for the WarpBackgroundService alias, idempotency guarded by early-return on duplicate T, instead of TryAddEnumerable.
deviations.justification: TryAddEnumerable rejects factory descriptors with identical ServiceType+ImplementationType (both WarpBackgroundService); guard achieves same de-dup guarantee.
